summary.headline: CRDO -10.51% with thesis-matched AI capex risk now materializing (Broadcom AI networking revenue missed estimates); broad semi selloff hit 6 more held names; CWAN delisted — roster cleanup required.

flags.headline: CRDO -10.51% as Broadcom AI networking miss signals hyperscaler capex moderation — thesis risk materializing
flags.detail: Credo Technology fell $28.22 (from $268.13 to $239.91) on Friday June 27. The catalyst: Broadcom reported AI networking revenue of $4.1B vs. $4.8B consensus estimate (a 14% miss), and CEO Hock Tan held his 2027 AI semiconductor revenue outlook flat rather than raising it. This repriced the entire hyperscaler AI capex narrative. CRDO derives nearly all revenue from AI data center connectivity (AECs, optical DSPs) and holds concentrated exposure to Microsoft, Google, Meta, and Amazon spending. The Broadcom miss is a direct read-through to CRDO demand. Analyst consensus remains bullish (10 strong buy / 15 buy / 2 hold) but the thesis risk has moved from hypothetical to active.
flags.thesis_link: Matches CRDO key_risk: 'AI capex cycle dependency — a slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending due to ROI concerns or macroeconomic headwinds would directly reduce demand for Credo's products given near-total AI exposure.'

evidence.source: Intellectia AI — Semiconductor Stocks Selloff June 2026
CNBC — Tech rout intensifies as sell-off grips global stocks

flags.headline: CWAN delisted June 25 — take-private closed at $24.55; remove from portfolios.json
flags.detail: The Clearwater Analytics take-private by Permira/Warburg Pincus/Francisco Partners/Temasek completed on June 25, 2026 at $24.55/share (FIRB clearance received June 19). CWAN is no longer a publicly traded equity. The data drop still shows it at $24.56 (settlement artifact). No position exists to monitor. portfolios.json growth sleeve still lists CWAN — this must be corrected so the sentinel monitors the correct 51-name universe.

evidence.source: Dashboard deal-watch.json — confirmed deal close June 25, FIRB June 19

flags.headline: Broad semi selloff on Broadcom capex miss: QCOM -8.0%, ADI -8.0%, NXPI -7.6%, KEYS -7.6%, LRCX -5.9%, MRVL -5.7%
flags.detail: Friday's Broadcom AI networking miss cascaded across the semiconductor book. Six held names fell 5-8%+ in a single session: QCOM ($188.62, -8.00%), ADI ($384.61, -7.99%), NXPI ($275.98, -7.65%), KEYS ($332.63, -7.64%), LRCX ($377.99, -5.93%), MRVL ($265.84, -5.69%). The iShares Semiconductor ETF was down ~6.2% on the day. Each of these names carries thesis-linked AI capex or semiconductor cyclicality risk. None had company-specific negative news — this is a sector reprice. The moves are material but not necessarily thesis-breaking on their own; they become significant if hyperscaler capex guidance continues to disappoint into the July earnings season.
flags.thesis_link: CRDO/MRVL: 'AI capex cycle moderation'; LRCX: 'WFE spending peak-to-trough swings if hyperscaler ROI disappoints'; KEYS: 'AI capex cycle dependency'; QCOM: semiconductor cyclicality; NXPI: US-China trade/tariff uncertainty amplifying moves.

evidence.source: Intellectia AI — Semiconductor Stocks Selloff June 2026

flags.headline: CAT -5.53% to $998 after ATH run — approaching $1,000 psychological level; tariff risk flag
flags.detail: Caterpillar fell $58.47 from $1,056.65 to $998.18, breaking below the $1,000 psychological level. CAT had reached an all-time high of $1,022.28 on June 22 (up ~60% YTD) driven by record $51B backlog and AI data center construction demand. The decline appears to be profit-taking following extreme overextension, potentially amplified by tariff concerns ($2.6B incremental tariff cost in 2026 is the known key risk). Not thesis-breaking at this level but the premium valuation (38.8x trailing P/E) leaves limited margin of safety. Watch for any deterioration in backlog or management tone.
flags.thesis_link: Matches CAT key_risk: '$2.6B in incremental tariff costs in 2026 that could compress adjusted operating profit margins' and 'Premium valuation at 38.8x trailing P/E leaves limited margin of safety if EPS growth fails to materialize.'

evidence.source: Tikr Blog — Caterpillar Stock up over 60% in 2026

flags.headline: Late-July hyperscaler earnings season is the desk's highest-priority macro risk signal for AI-exposed names
flags.detail: NVDA, MRVL, AMD, TSM, CRDO, LRCX, KEYS, ADI are all materially exposed to hyperscaler capex. The desk considers late-July 2026 hyperscaler prints (Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta, Amazon) the single most important macro signal for these names. Broadcom's guidance miss on June 27 has pre-loaded anxiety into the tape. On the day after each hyperscaler print, this sentinel will summarize capex tone for affected holdings. Calendar entries being tracked separately below.
flags.thesis_link: Standing watch item per RISK_SENTINEL_PROMPT.md Step 4.

These two positions are not listed in portfolios.json (which shows 50 names: 25 dividend, 25 growth). The firm describes 52 holdings across Dividend, Growth, and a Digital-Asset-ETF sleeve. Until portfolios.json is updated to include the digital sleeve, the sentinel's roster-level checks are incomplete for these two positions.

flags.headline: SYK +5.11% on OptaBlate patent win and 4.8% dividend increase
flags.detail: Stryker gained $16.15 (from $316.15 to $332.30) on a federal court ruling in its favor on the OptaBlate patent dispute. The company also announced a 4.8% Q3 2026 dividend increase. Catalysts on track; thesis not stressed.

flags.headline: HOOD +5.62% on Bitstamp acquisition close, new crypto product launch July 2, and analyst Buy initiation
flags.detail: Robinhood gained $5.25 (from $93.46 to $98.71) driven by: (1) Bitstamp acquisition closing, significantly strengthening crypto product suite; (2) announcement of a 'next-generation crypto financial product' launching July 2; (3) analyst Buy initiation at $125 price target on June 26; (4) $2B convertible notes with ~$290M earmarked for share repurchases. Approaching the $100 psychological level. Catalysts ahead of plan.

evidence.source: Yahoo Finance — HOOD products, diversification, crypto 2026

flags.headline: Crypto-exposed names rally Friday: COIN +4.69%, HUT +4.38%, MARA +4.18%, IBIT +0.87%, ETHA +1.11%
flags.detail: Bitcoin in $60K-$62K range on June 27, driving gains across crypto-adjacent holdings. COIN ($149.20, +4.69%), HUT ($122.78, +4.38%), MARA ($14.47, +4.18%). Digital ETF sleeve calm (IBIT +0.87%, ETHA +1.11%). No company-specific catalysts beyond broad crypto sentiment. Note: HUT and MARA are high-volatility crypto miners — moves of this magnitude are normal for these names.

flags.headline: CWAN take-private closed cleanly at $24.55 on June 25 — no spread blow-up, no FIRB issues
flags.detail: The Clearwater Analytics merger-arb position resolved exactly as underwritten. FIRB clearance received June 19, deal closed June 25 at the full $24.55/share terms. No haircut, no last-minute regulatory friction. This is the thesis working as intended for a merger-arb position.

deal_watch.terms: $24.55 take-private (Permira / Warburg Pincus / Francisco Partners / Temasek)
deal_watch.note: Deal closed at full terms June 25. FIRB received June 19. No open gates. Remove CWAN from portfolios.json. No further monitoring needed.
